recent_evidence.summary: Second Ramsey autosave opportunity review: The det=1.0 MHz r03 Ramsey/T2star/13C follow-up remains running. A 5/8 stored-average autosave raw export was reviewed while live status had advanced to 6/8 averages, final-count text 38.253 kcps, monitor error empty, and no stop request. The partial screen is still not claim-grade: the largest combined component is near 1.166 MHz, while the programmed 1.0 MHz carrier and expected 13C sidebands remain smaller. No hard anomaly, no T2star or 13C claim; continue to terminal raw/readout, scan-order-aware drift, and FFT/least-squares review.

Terminal raw/readout, scan-order-aware drift, FFT, and least-squares review for det=1.0 MHz r03 Ramsey follow-up: job completed safely with 8 x 50000 shots per tau point, final counts 44.184 kcps, no monitor error or stop request, and no drift flags. The programmed 1.0 MHz carrier is weak (ratio LS amplitude 0.00916; raw signal 0.277 kcps, below the expected 2-6 kcps scale and below/near SEM), the largest combined component is near 1.178 MHz rather than the carrier or expected 13C sidebands, and per-average frequency screens are inconsistent. No supported T2star or nearby-13C claim from this terminal Ramsey; next useful branch is a redesigned short-tau/high-SNR Ramsey or alternate diagnostic, not a blind repeat.

Short-tau/high-SNR Ramsey diagnostic design after two non-claim-grade r03 Ramsey runs: scan tau 48 ns..1.968 us in 41 sample-grid points, det=1.0 MHz, mw_freq=3.8759 GHz, 12 x 90000 repetitions (1.08e6 shots per tau point), explicit r03 position. Quantitative model projects median raw SEM about 1.17 kcps vs 1.92 kcps in terminal 8 us run and targets the specific very-short-T2*/early-time carrier failure mode rather than blind repeat. MATLAB wrapper preview/advisory returned ok with no blockers; estimated per-average/tracking window 577.339 s under the 900 s nighttime cap, total runtime 6953 s scheduling-only. Experiment intent verified with bridge queued/running empty.

Queued and started the intended short-tau/high-SNR Ramsey diagnostic. Due to a relaunch race, the actual running bridge job id is nv23_ramsey_20260513_230331_auto_ramsey (generic suffix) but job metadata links it to verified intent image145844_reimage_r03_ramsey_shorttau_48ns_1968ns_12x90k_20260513_2257. It uses auto__ramsey, tau 48 ns..1.968 us in 41 points, mw_freq=3.8759 GHz, det=1.0 MHz, 12 x 90000 reps, explicit r03 position. Runtime status at copy time was running average 1/12, monitor last_error empty, final-count text 44.184 kcps. A duplicate queued relaunch job nv23_ramsey_20260513_230511_image145844_reimage_r03_ramsey_shorttau_48ns_1968ns_12x90k was canceled before execution by moving queued/ -> canceled/ with a cancellation note; bridge queued is now empty and one intended job remains running.

Focused work note for the short-tau/high-SNR r03 Ramsey diagnostic design, start, and duplicate queued relaunch cleanup. It records inputs read, measurement settings, advisory/runtime estimates, actual running job id, canceled duplicate id, checks performed, and terminal-review requirements.
